step_by_step_thinking: I agree with the consensus that Hodgkin's disease is correctly classified as a malignant neoplasm (Option D). One additional point to emphasize is the immunohistochemical staining patterns often observed in Reed-Sternberg cells, which further confirm their abnormality compared to normal B-cells. These markers help distinguish them from other types of lymphomas and solidify the diagnosis of Hodgkin's disease as a distinct entity among cancers.
raw_response:
I agree with the consensus that Hodgkin's disease is correctly classified as a malignant neoplasm (Option D). One additional point to emphasize is the immunohistochemical staining patterns often observed in Reed-Sternberg cells, which further confirm their abnormality compared to normal B-cells. These markers help distinguish them from other types of lymphomas and solidify the diagnosis of Hodgkin's disease as a distinct entity among cancers. 

Answer: D
